title: Histories III.80-82: The Debate on Government
description: Three Persian nobles debate democracy, oligarchy, and monarchy — the first recorded political theory.
introduction: Herodotus records a debate among three Persian conspirators — Otanes, Megabyzus, and Darius — about the best form of government. This is the earliest known systematic comparison of democracy, oligarchy, and monarchy in Western literature, predating Plato's Republic and Aristotle's Politics by a century. Each speaker identifies the fatal flaw in the others' preferred system. The passage shaped all subsequent Western political thought.

translation.original_text: Ὀτάνης μὲν ἐκέλευε ἐς μέσον Πέρσῃσι καταθεῖναι τὰ πρήγματα... πλῆθος δὲ ἄρχον πρῶτα μὲν οὔνομα πάντων κάλλιστον ἔχει, ἰσονομίην.
translation.text: Otanes urged them to put the government in the hands of the whole Persian people... Rule by the many has first the fairest name of all: equality before the law.

translation.original_text: ἑνὸς τοῦ ἀρίστου ἀνδρὸς οὐδὲν ἄμεινον ἂν φανείη· γνώμῃ γὰρ τοιαύτῃ χρεώμενος ἐπιτροπεύοι ἂν ἀμωμήτως τοῦ πλήθεος.
translation.text: Nothing better can appear than the rule of the one best man. With such judgment he would govern the people blamelessly.
